method_description: User clicks START on the Remove My Info page, selects state from dropdown, completes reCAPTCHA v2, reviews a state-specific privacy law notice (click GOT IT, CONTINUE), then fills an opt-out form with name, address, phone number, and email address fields (only info currently listed on SpyDialer). Clicks OPT OUT MY INFO to submit. Immediate on-screen confirmation — no email verification or SMS required.
bot_check: reCAPTCHA v2 image challenge at Step 1 (state selection page). Confirmed reCAPTCHA v2 — checkbox triggers visual image recognition challenges (buses, bicycles, motorcycles, cars). Always required, cannot be bypassed.
profile_used: James Smith, 1541 Mims St SW, Birmingham, AL 35211, age 63 — searched via PEOPLE tab with name + city + state. Profile match verification was attempted but could not be confirmed due to CAPTCHA blocking the opt-out submission flow.
email_used: Not tested — could not complete opt-out form due to reCAPTCHA blocking automated completion
notes: Opt-out URL found immediately in footer via 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' and 'Remove My Info' links — both point to /Consumers/. PEOPLE search tab supports name + city + state fields as required by PIR. Search results stay on spydialer.com domain. reCAPTCHA v2 confirmed at Step 1 (supported type). Could not get past reCAPTCHA v2 image challenges to verify the full opt-out form, so Tier 2 checks (form fields, generated info acceptance, confirmation flow) are untested. Third-party guides (Optery, Guaranteed Removals) describe the form as requesting name, address, phone, email with immediate on-screen confirmation and no email verification, but this was not directly verified. California vs Texas showed same flow through Tier 1 — CAPTCHA blocked comparison beyond Step 1. Broker warns data may reappear from public record updates.
